supportLevel: Unofficial public Microsoft Fabric status mirror based on HTML scraping

active.detailsHtml: Fabric customers using the Teams mobile app may experience issues loading reports within the app. As a workaround, customers may open the reports in a browser or the Power BI app. A fix is expected to be implemented globally by 2026-06-02 11:00 PDT.

resolved.detailsHtml:
Power BI has reenabled external email send functionality for limited set of customers on a case-by-case basis. If your tenant is still impacted and wish to request re-enablement, please generate a support request for assistance.

A mitigation is in validation phases and the tenant level switch for external recipients should be respected again by 2026-05-01 17:00 PDT.
resolved.detailsText: Power BI has reenabled external email send functionality for limited set of customers on a case-by-case basis. If your tenant is still impacted and wish to request re-enablement, please generate a support request for assistance. A mitigation is in validation phases and the tenant level switch for external recipients should be respected again by 2026-05-01 17:00 PDT.

resolved.detailsHtml:
Fabric customers may have experienced issues opening semantic models from My Workspace and accessing semantic models associated with shared reports in the service when users do not have access to the workspace. 
As a workaround, user can do below:
  1. Click on View Details of the report in My Workspace, selecting the Lineage tab, choosing the semantic model under Lineage and related items, and opening it.
  2. Users can access the semantic model through the OneLake Catalog by opening the report, selecting the Lineage tab, choosing the semantic model under Lineage and related items, and opening it.
resolved.detailsText: a { text-decoration: none; color: #464feb;}tr th, tr td { border: 1px solid #e6e6e6;}tr th { background-color: #f5f5f5;} Fabric customers may have experienced issues opening semantic models from My Workspace and accessing semantic models associated with shared reports in the service when users do not have access to the workspace. As a workaround, user can do below: Click on View Details of the report in My Workspace, selecting the Lineage tab, choosing the semantic model under Lineage and related items, and opening it. Users can access the semantic model through the OneLake Catalog by opening the report, selecting the Lineage tab, choosing the semantic model under Lineage and related items, and opening it.

resolved.detailsHtml: Power BI customers may observe that the subscription email template has been altered, and reverting to the original template is underway. Customers may see additional revisions to this template in the future, and it is recommended to update processes to be resilient to these changes.  We apologize for the disruption this change has caused. Future updates to this template will be communicated with notice so customers can update processes appropriately.

resolved.detailsHtml: The service disruption in the Norway West region, along with global latency and performance issues caused by an Azure thermal incident, has been successfully resolved. As of December 6th at 03:10 AM PST, Microsoft Fabric services have been fully restored, and customers now have complete access to all functionalities. A thorough root cause analysis is underway, and preventative measures will be implemented to enhance system stability and minimize future disruptions
resolved.detailsText: The service disruption in the Norway West region, along with global latency and performance issues caused by an Azure thermal incident, has been successfully resolved. As of December 6th at 03:10 AM PST, Microsoft Fabric services have been fully restored, and customers now have complete access to all functionalities. A thorough root cause analysis is underway, and preventative measures will be implemented to enhance system stability and minimize future disruptions

resolved.detailsHtml:
Fabric customers may experience issues with Semantic Model Direct Query, Model and Dataflow Gen1 refreshes. Engineers have identified the root cause, and a mitigation has been applied to remediate the impact.

 

Note: If the scheduled refresh is disabled for the models due to consecutive failures during the impact period, users can re-enable it back from Dataset Refresh settings page. Please refer to the public documentation for more information: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/refresh-data
resolved.detailsText: Fabric customers may experience issues with Semantic Model Direct Query, Model and Dataflow Gen1 refreshes. Engineers have identified the root cause, and a mitigation has been applied to remediate the impact. Note : If the scheduled refresh is disabled for the models due to consecutive failures during the impact period, users can re-enable it back from Dataset Refresh settings page. Please refer to the public documentation for more information: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/connect-data/refresh-data

resolved.detailsHtml:
Power BI customers using Desktop version starting from June 2025 (2.144.679.0) for reports with distinct count function may experience issues while rendering their visuals using Snowflake connector implementation 2.0 with DirectQuery. As a mitigation, customers can specify "Implementation="1.0" in their M queries. Engineers have identified the root cause, and a fix is expected to be deployed by end of the day 08/05/2025.

 

For more details, please visit: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/connectors/snowflake
resolved.detailsText: Power BI customers using Desktop version starting from June 2025 (2.144.679.0) for reports with distinct count function may experience issues while rendering their visuals using Snowflake connector implementation 2.0 with DirectQuery. As a mitigation, customers can specify "Implementation="1.0" in their M queries. Engineers have identified the root cause, and a fix is expected to be deployed by end of the day 08/05/2025. For more details, please visit: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-query/connectors/snowflake

resolved.detailsHtml:
The service disruption in the France Central region, originating from an Azure Storage–related incident, has been successfully resolved. As of August 4th at 6:40 AM UTC+2 (Central European Summer Time), Microsoft Fabric services have been fully restored, and customers now have complete access to all functionalities.


If users notice that data has not been refreshed, they can initiate a manual refresh to resume processing. A thorough root cause analysis is underway, and preventive measures will be implemented to enhance system stability and reduce the risk of future disruptions.
resolved.detailsText: The service disruption in the France Central region, originating from an Azure Storage–related incident, has been successfully resolved. As of August 4th at 6:40 AM UTC+2 (Central European Summer Time), Microsoft Fabric services have been fully restored, and customers now have complete access to all functionalities. If users notice that data has not been refreshed, they can initiate a manual refresh to resume processing. A thorough root cause analysis is underway, and preventive measures will be implemented to enhance system stability and reduce the risk of future disruptions.

resolved.detailsHtml:
Fabric customers might experience data discrepancies when running queries against their SQL endpoints. As a workaround, users can follow below steps to mitigate. Engineers have identified the root cause and a fix is expected to be deployed by end-of-day 07/30/2025.


1. For non-shortcut tables:

a) Rename the impacted table to a new name
b) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint
c) Rename the impacted table back to the original name
d) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint'

 

2. For shortcut tables:

a) Drop the shortcut table from LakeHouse
b) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint
c) Create a new shortcut table from LakeHouse
d) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint
resolved.detailsText: Fabric customers might experience data discrepancies when running queries against their SQL endpoints. As a workaround, users can follow below steps to mitigate. Engineers have identified the root cause and a fix is expected to be deployed by end-of-day 07/30/2025. 1. For non-shortcut tables: a) Rename the impacted table to a new name b) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint c) Rename the impacted table back to the original name d) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint' 2. For shortcut tables: a) Drop the shortcut table from LakeHouse b) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint c) Create a new shortcut table from LakeHouse d) Execute Metadata Sync from SQL Endpoint
